name: 17q21_31_KANSL1_inversion_SD
description: KANSL1-flanking SDs (sub-locus of 17q21.31 inversion polymorphism). Two ~200 kb LCR17q21 blocks flank KANSL1 in inverted orientation on the H1 haplotype and in direct orientation on H2, explaining why microdeletions are restricted to H2 carriers (NAHR substrate alignment).

architecture.internal_structure: LCR17q21A (proximal) and LCR17q21B (distal) of ~200 kb at >99% identity; H1 = inverted (NAHR-protected), H2 = direct (NAHR-permissive); KANSL1 unique core between the LCRs.
